statement.markdown: Write a function that distributes a certain number of cookies fairly among a group of students. The number of cookies and the number of students should be given as input to the function, which should return True if the distribution is fair and False otherwise. A distribution is fair if and only if each student gets the same number of cookies, except possibly for one student who gets one more cookie than all the others. For example, distributing 12 cookies among 5 students with 2 cookies each and 1 student with 3 cookies would be considered fair.
statement.inputFormat: The function takes three parameters: the number of cookies, the number of students, and a list of the number of cookies each student has.
statement.outputFormat: The function should return a boolean indicating whether the distribution is fair.
statement.notes: The input values can be arbitrary positive integers.

examples.explanation: Distribution is fair because all students but one have the same number of cookies, and the exception has one more cookie.
Distribution is fair because all students have the same number of cookies.
Distribution is not fair because more than one student has a different number of cookies.

hints.text: Use sorting as a potential solution.

editorial.title: Fair Distribution of Cookies
editorial.intuition: Sort the cookies each student has and check the difference between consecutive elements.
editorial.bruteForce: Sort the cookies each student has and check if all students but one have the same number of cookies.
editorial.optimalSolution: Sort the cookies each student has and check if the difference between consecutive elements is at most one. Return true if this condition is met, false otherwise.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The solution is correct because a distribution is fair only if each student gets the same number of cookies, except possibly for one student who gets one more cookie than all the others.
commonMistakes: Forgetting to handle the case where all students have the same number of cookies

functionSignature.cpp: bool fairDistribution(int cookies, int students, int studentCookies[]);
functionSignature.java: boolean fairDistribution(int cookies, int students, int[] studentCookies);
functionSignature.python: bool fairDistribution(self, cookies: int, students: int, studentCookies: list[int]) -> bool:
